issue.title: Idempotency bug in escaped semicolon in Markdown link
issue.body: Prettier v3.9.5. With --parser markdown, `[test](/hello?foo&quot\;bar)` formats on the first pass to `[test](/hello?foo"bar)` and on the second pass to `[test](/hello?foo"bar)`, because the escaped semicolon guarding the `"` character reference loses its backslash and then resolves as an entity. Expected: the escaped form `[test](/hello?foo&quot\;bar)` is preserved (idempotent). Reporter gives the reproduction and expected output.

note: Live recheck at fetch time and re-verified hours later before receipt/push: OPEN, unassigned, labels help-wanted + lang:markdown intact; ZERO comments, ZERO claims, ZERO cross-referenced or open PRs. Fresh issue (created 2026-07-10). No CLA/DCO required (MIT). Clean, uncontested target.
